title: podman kill fails to kill container, podman ps lists dead container
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a container which hangs (sorry, not great, what I did at the time was try to paste hundreds of lines into Vim which was running in a container, I can reproduce this repeatedly).
2. Run `podman kill <container>`, it exits success
3. Run `podman ps`, the container is still up
4. Run `kill -KILL` on the `conmon` process associated with the above container
5. Run `podman ps`, the container is still listed with status “Up”
6. Run `podman kill <containers>`, get error:
    > kill container: No such process
    > Error: error sending signal to container <id>: `/usr/bin/crun kill <id> 9` failed: exit status 1`

The second time I tried to reproduce this, after the first `podman kill`, `podman kill` would give
> kill container: No such process
except the process *was* still alive for the relevant container ID.

**Describe the results you received:**

See above.

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman kill` should actually kill, `podman ps` should not list containers which are dead (or should list them so)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.4
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.6
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
